characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A Dog Named "Dog": A majority of their names are exactly their species. Talking Animal: Most of them are capable of speaking.

characters.name: Skunk
characters.description: The main hero of the series who strives to be a Kung Fu master by being taught by his Master Panda. Because Destiny Says So: Skunk is destined to defeat Dragon. Bratty Half-Pint: He’ll act like this sometimes and get quite mischievous. Butt-Monkey: He can have a lot of bad luck despite being the hero. The Chosen One: He was given to Panda when he asked for help defeating Dragon.

characters.name: Master Panda
characters.description: Master Panda is the teacher of Skunk and the leader of the Valley Animals. Badass Teacher: He spends most of his time teaching Skunk kung fu but he will get into battle and fights really well. Beary Friendly: With someBeware the Nice Onesmoments. Big Eater: A big panda with a big appetite. Big Good: Since he is the leader of the Valley Animals.

characters.name: Rabbit
characters.description: Rabbit is one of the many allies and friends Skunk has, though he can be quite a jerk and a bully towards him at times. Anti-Hero: He's arrogant, cocky, short-tempered, mean, and violent who picks on Skunk and has a big ego, but is on the side of good. Badass Adorable: Like Skunk, he's a furry little fellow who can kick ass. Badass Boast: He manages one when Baboon asks the classic "You and what army?" Rabbit's response? He flexes one arm, then the other, saying, "This army over here, and thisarmyover here. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Fox.

characters.name: Fox
characters.description: Fox is another one of Skunk’s friends and allies. She is almost like a big sister to him and is Rabbit’s crush. Action Girl: The most action-oriented female in the show. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Rabbit. Cool Big Sister: Fox to Skunk. She was taken in by Panda when her father was killed, and seems to view Skunk as a little brother. Cunning Like a Fox: So much so that she’s one of the more intelligent Valley Animals.

characters.name: Pig
characters.description: Pig is a wild boar who Skunk occasionally hangs out with and is often ditzy and a bit of a slouch in the brains department. Beware the Silly Ones: Despite his ditziness he can still put up quite a fight, especially if its truffle season. Big Eater: He has moments of a big appetite. Cloudcuckoolander: Not very bright and often in la-la-land. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: He becomes "Master Pig", thanks to Rabbit's "octopus arm" weapon (a plunger attached to a rope, which Pig excels in using).

characters.name: Ms. Duck
characters.description: Ms. Duck is a wise old bird who is good friends with Panda. She is also a master at cooking and Dim Sum Fu. Beware the Nice Ones: She’s usually portrayed as a nice old lady but she can still hold her own in battle. Cool Old Lady: She knows how to fight when she does. Never Mess with Granny: She’s an old lady but still an effective fighter. Paper Fan of Doom: She, Fox, Crane and Skunk are part of their own fan-fighting group.

characters.name: Doctor Turtle
characters.description: Doctor Turtle is, as you can tell, the doctor of the Valley Animals. He’s also good friends with Panda. Beware the Nice Ones: Much like Duck he is usually a nice old man but can still be fierce when triggered. The Medic: He isn’t called "Doctor" Turtle for nothing.

characters.name: Tiger
characters.description: Tiger is another warrior of the Valley Animals, though he tends to be too much of a coward to really fight. Berserk Button: In "The Art Of Being A Pebble" he gets angry and fights the Ninja Monkeys when they destroy his valuables. Butt-Monkey: Has his moments though not as much as other characters. Cats Are Mean: Averted since he’s a mighty warrior and part of the good side. Cowardly Lion: Or Cowardly Tiger in this case.

characters.name: Snake
characters.description: Snake is the spy of the Valley Animals. Butt-Monkey: He can either suffer incidents or just be the butt of jokes. Just a Stupid Accent: His Australian accent. Snakes Are Sinister: Averted, though he does retain some sly snake characteristics. Especially averted in that he's a cobra, the kind of snake most often associated with this trope.

characters.name: Crane
characters.description: Crane is the valley gossip. Gossipy Hen: It’s her role in the series. Older Than They Look: Doesn’t appear particularly old but is considered a valley elder along with Duck, Turtle and Panda. She even knew Duck when she was a Duckling. Paper Fan of Doom: She, Fox, Duck and Skunk are part of their own fan-fighting group.

characters.name: Mantis
characters.description: Mantis is another warrior of the Valley, albeit one of the weaker ones. Butt-Monkey: Most of the time, though sometimeshe quite enjoys it. I Just Want to Be Special: So much so that he was extremely happy to trade his destiny with Skunk in one episode. No Celebrities Were Harmed: OfChristopher Walken.

characters.name: Ox and Bird
characters.description: Ox and Bird are a duo of animals that, unlike the others, spend their time slacking off and lazying about. Which makes for a great pair of best friends for Skunk. Big Guy, Little Guy: Bird is the small guy and Ox is the big guy. Butt-Monkey: Bird has these moments from time to time. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: Ox’s ditziness also means he has no thoughts, which actually comes in handy for seeing the Ninja Monkeys when they’re invisible. Deadpan Snarker: Bird likes to make a lot of wisecracks.

characters.name: Frog
characters.description: Frog is the master of jumping. He is one of the lesser used characters in the show. Fun Person Ified: Often the most happy and jolly of the characters.

characters.name: Fish
characters.description: Fish is the master of swimming. Badass Teacher: Right up there with Panda.

characters.name: The Killer Bees
characters.description: The Killer Bees are a nest of bees that will attack anyone who provokes them. Anti-Hero: Zig-zagged. Sometimes they seem completely neutral and will attack anyone who provokes them (good or bad) but other times they can be allies with the other animals. Bee Afraid: Only when provoked. Hip-Hop: They speak in hip-hip style of dialogue. Insect Gender-Bender: The swarm consists of worker bees but they seem to be all male.

characters.name: Dragon
characters.description: Dragon is theBig Badof the series. He use to be a noble protector of all the animals but after using water without permission from Heaven to prevent a drought he was punished and imprisoned. Now he seeks vengeance on all the animals for his damnation. Anti-Villain: He could be seen as this as he only became evil due to how severe his punishment was. Big Bad: Of the series. Body Horror: As a result of his punishment from Heaven, Dragon's skin is so badly burnt he cannot leave the icy lake in his cave. Evil Is Bigger: Well he is a dragon.

characters.name: Baboon
characters.description: Baboon is the main lackey of Dragon and the head of the Ninja Monkey army. Butt-Monkey: Often his failures lead him to being humiliated. Camp Straight: Despite his sometimes effeminate actions, he obviously has a crush on Fox. Catchphrase: "Wasabi!" The Dragon: Which is ironic given who hisBig Badis.

characters.name: The Ninja Monkeys
characters.description: Butt Monkeys: Both literally and metaphorically. Whether it be losing to the Valley Animals embarrassingly or being abused by their boss Baboon. Jerkass: One monkey in particular (identified by a goat-like beard) was like this to Skunk in the episode "The Art Of Revenge" where he smashed his green tea cake (prompting Skunk to want revenge), though he later had aPet the Dogmoment where lets Skunk and Rabbit go (after capturing them) when Skunk decided not to get revenge by smashing his banana pie. Maniac Monkeys: They are the mainMooksafter all. Mooks: Of the series. Not So Harmless Villains: They are usually too dimwitted to come close to winning but they can still be competent lackeys at times.
